title: The Culture Reset
content:
Health systems are under intense pressure, but what do the people inside the organization say is really breaking down? The public conversation centers on staffing shortages, burnout, and the daily strain of delivering care. Inside the walls, a more revealing story is taking shape.

Culture is becoming the deciding factor for whether teams stay connected, stay committed, and stay.

Based on new research from BPD, The Culture Reset surfaces a clear tension. Many employees still believe in the mission, yet a meaningful amount feel less supported than they need to be, and a significant portion say they are at least somewhat likely to leave in the near future. Physicians, in particular, signal sustained dissatisfaction. The guide outlines a practical path forward, with high-level actions leaders can take to rebuild trust, restore purpose, strengthen peer connection, and use communication to make progress visible.

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide.

Download this guide to explore:

1

The role physician leaders can play as the most credible force for culture change

2

 What visible follow-through looks like when you are rebuilding trust

3

Ways to make outcomes and impact tangible so purpose stays front and center

4

How to communicate workload relief in clear, role-specific terms

5

 Why audience-based messaging lands differently for physicians, leaders, and staff

title: Crossing The Einstein Divide
content:
The headlines around AI primarily focus on saving time and costs. And in the rush to automate, we’ve noticed the conversation is focused almost exclusively on efficiency and effectiveness.

But what if that’s only the beginning?

This paper aims to inspire marketers to dream bigger. It’s an invitation to ask, “What can we do that’s never been done before?” To answer that question, it helps to first imagine what that future could look like.

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide. Download this guide to explore:

1

The shift from “Edison mode” to “Einstein mode”—why imagination beats automation.

2

AI thought experiments that rewrite campaigns, the “digital front door,” and real-time messaging.

3

Simple mental models—lateral thinking, beautiful questions, first-principles—to spark braver ideas.

4

A CMO playbook for the AI era: how to redesign teams and prove it with the right metrics.

title: The Courage to Save Millions
content:
Health system leaders are always on the lookout for ideas and strategies from outside the industry to advance their organization.

Here’s one worth a closer look, a strategy that strengthens culture, enhances reputation and market share, and can add millions to the bottom line. It hinges on a single decision made at the right time, one that most leaders overlook during mergers and acquisitions.

That’s why BPD and BrandActive created The Courage to Save Millions — a guide to making the brand identity decision that could transform your next M&A deal into millions in EBITDA savings.

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide.

Download this guide to explore:

1

Clarity on a commonly overlooked decision—why health systems miss it, and how recognizing it early can save millions in EBITDA

 

2

Uncover the cultural and operational strategy that builds brand loyalty, boosts market share, and strengthens internal culture

3

Avoid the costly mistake most health leaders make—and discover how to seize a high-impact opportunity when it matters most

title: Good people, flawed system
content:
Health insurance companies are under growing scrutiny—but what do the people inside those organizations think?

While the public conversation focuses on rising costs and care delays, a more nuanced story is emerging from within. This report explores the growing moral tension faced by payor employees: a workforce that believes in its mission but is increasingly uneasy about how that mission plays out in practice.

Based on a national survey by BPD, Good People, Flawed System reveals a surprising contradiction. Employees of health insurance companies report high job satisfaction and strong alignment with their organizations’ values—yet 67% also have concerns over how their organizations prioritize profits over patients. These insights offer providers a powerful new lens for negotiating with payors and building more mission-aligned partnerships.

Download Report

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the report.

Download this report to explore:

1

Why employees believe in their organizations—despite rising ethical concerns

2

How internal tension is shaping perceptions of access, care delays, and cost

3

Ways health systems can use this data to build more effective, values-aligned partnerships with payors

title: The Future of the CMO
content:
Marketing’s value in health systems is under siege.

What began as a quiet elimination of CMO roles across top health systems is now a full-blown reckoning: healthcare economics, financial pressures, and the rise of AI are forcing healthcare marketers to face new realities, or face dwindling influence, budgets and job security. In 2023, BPD called this moment “Rome is Burning.” By 2025, it’s clear the threats are real, and growing. What’s also clear is that opportunities for CMOs and those in marketing are also real. This report is based on two years of research and conversations with CMOS, CEOs and other executives in the health system sector, including conference panels, the 2024 Joe Public Retreat, and qualitative interviews with more than three dozen executives.

Download Report

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the report. Download this report to explore:

1

A deep dive into the new threats health system CMOs face

2

Five opportunities for rising above today’s upheaval and building a stronger platform for marketing

3

What CEOs think about the value of marketing, and how their views will define your future

title: Tomorrow Is Too Late, the BPD Value Attack Series
content:
The growing perception that hospitals prioritize revenue over patients is now defining the industry. BPD defined this as the "value attack" in early 2023, and since that time, it has only intensified, posing real operational and reputational threats in both statehouses and public opinion. Health systems must do more — now — to address these threats. Tomorrow is too late.

For nearly a decade, insurers, policymakers, and the public have framed hospitals as caring more about reimbursement and dollars than patient well-being, with occasional acts of charity seen as PR moves.

 

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide.

Download this white paper to gain insights on:

1

Proactively reshape your hospital’s narrative to combat the growing perception that profits outweigh patient care

2

Build alliances with trusted voices like physicians and community leaders to defend your hospital against damaging public narratives

3

Engage in coordinated national efforts to push back on false claims from insurers and policymakers targeting healthcare providers

4

Engage in coordinated national efforts to push back on false claims from insurers and policymakers targeting healthcare providers

title: The Case for Building a Post Health System Brand
content:
For those who are ready, a post health system brand will help you connect with consumers on a deeper level and beat the competition at its own game.

We’ve heard a million times about the impact of growing consumerism and new competition from players like Apple, Amazon and CVS. The combination of these two forces shines a harsh light on health system brands. What’s worked in the past for health system branding will likely not be enough moving forward. For example, the point has repeatedly been made that consumers are not just comparing your health system brand to other health systems, they are comparing it to Uber, Amazon and Starbucks. If that’s true, then why are health systems still the same old health system brands?

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide.

One solution is moving to what we call a post health system brand.

For those who are ready, a post health system brand will help you connect with consumers on a deeper level and beat the competition at its own game. There is some urgency to this. As we highlight in this paper, those who move first can own a market, while the rest will be left to follow. Additionally, the challenges are significant, and many systems are not yet ready to make the move. But employed successfully, a post health system brand won’t just help you become the top health system brand in your market. It will help you become a top brand, period.

title: Selling Bold Ideas to Health System Leadership
content:
Ready to get your bold marketing idea across the finish line? Here's what you need to know to get the green light from health system leadership.

With stakeholders married to the traditional, tight grips on thinning margins and a risk-averse audience to sway, health system marketers have their work cut out for them when selling bold ideas internally. But to truly build leading brands in healthcare, systems must engage with consumers in new and compelling ways that bring them closer to your system for their health.

 

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide.

Ready to get started? Download this guide for insights on:

1

Common hurdles marketers encounter when selling up

2

What motivates health system leadership to push back

3

How to pave the way for bold marketing long-term

4

Pitching strategies to get your bold idea approved

title: How Consumers Navigate Healthcare
content:
Healthcare marketers need to get ahead of the curve when it comes to the future of customer engagement and experiences.

In the complex world of healthcare, marketers face a unique challenge: Consumers don’t behave like consumers. So, what’s a marketer to do? How do you nudge consumers to choose your brand when they are not fully in control of their own journey?

 
 

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide.

Download this guide to see the patient journey through three distinct phases:

1

Living moments: when consumers are just going about their daily lives, not thinking about clinical care, doctors, hospitals - or even their health.

2

Thinking moments: when consumers are actively considering their health or the health of their loved ones, researching specific topics and even exploring symptoms or more general conditions online.

3

Solving moments: when consumers actively seek healthcare solutions.

title: The Healthcare Value Attack
content:
The time is now to defend your hospital or health system from the trend that we call the value attack: a false narrative that detracts from the tangible value your organization provides to your community.

Even if your system hasn’t been criticized aloud, your reputation is suffering. Consumers, politicians and the media are watching healthcare with a skeptical eye as costs rise and care becomes less accessible. We conducted research with 1,200 consumers to understand what consumers think about hospitals, health systems and their priorities, and our findings uncovered insights that your organization needs to change the narrative with actionable steps to weather the storm.

Download Guide

Fill out the form below to receive a copy of the guide.

Download this white paper to gain insights on:

1

Being proactive against media attacks with a story built on your organization’s indispensability as a healthcare provider

2

Reversing the trend of growing institutional mistrust among consumers looking for the care they need

3

Defending yourself against the perception of hospitals and health systems as profit-hungry

4

Showing the real and positive impact your organization has had on your community
